mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in over 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample testing with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during early-cycle expansions, or the correlation coefficient between INDPRO growth and 20-day returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where payroll growth exceeds 100000, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates sustained aggregate demand, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when GDP growth falls below 2%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, triggering defensive portfolio shifts and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production declines, or the mechanism out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40% during late-cycle periods.
